notes.anchor: had not Pansa fiercely stood against it
notes.note: C. Vibius Pansa, one of the two consuls of 43 BC; as presiding magistrate he could block Cicero's motion in the Senate. He died of wounds soon after the battles around Mutina.

notes.anchor: I was led out to a public meeting by the tribune of the plebs M. Servilius
notes.note: A contio was a non-voting public assembly; only a magistrate (here the tribune Servilius) could convene one and "lead out" a speaker to address the people. Tribunes of the plebs held this power as a key lever of popular politics.
The wealthy Roman province of western Anatolia, a prize Cicero assumes Cassius can secure for the republican cause along with the rest of the East.
